data.fix: Update the pod's image to a valid one using the command `kubectl patch pod imagepull-pod -p '{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"imagepull-pod","image":"valid-repo/valid-image:latest"}]}}'` or modify the deployment configuration to use a correct image.

The pod is failing due to a "config not found" error, indicating that the application is unable to locate its required configuration.

data.root_cause: The pod is failing due to a "config not found" error, indicating that the application is unable to locate its required configuration.
data.fix: kubectl set env deployment/crash-loop-pod -n default CONFIG_FILE=/path/to/config/file
data.explanation: The application is consistently crashing and restarting, as indicated by the high restart count, due to the inability to find its configuration. The "FATAL: config not found" message in the pod logs confirms this. To resolve the issue, the configuration file needs to be made available to the application, either by mounting a volume or setting an environment variable pointing to the config file.

data.fix: kubectl describe pod crash-loop-pod -n default && kubectl logs crash-loop-pod -n default --previous && kubectl edit pod crash-loop-pod -n default to troubleshoot and adjust the pod configuration as needed.
